category_title: Students with CHARGE syndrome exhibiting challenging behaviors (kicking, biting, scratching) to escape tasks or due to lack of structure
minimal_context: David is asked to complete routine table work involving sorting objects at the start of the school day.
dialogue:
TUTOR: David, it is time to start the day. Please sit at the table and begin sorting these objects.

STUDENT: (David does not sit at the table. He begins jumping and grinding his teeth.)

TUTOR: Let's get to work. Look at the objects on the table.

STUDENT: (David engages in visual self-stimulatory behavior, ignoring the sorting task.)

TUTOR: You need to do your table work now. Sit down.

STUDENT: (David exhibits problematic behaviors to escape the task, scratching at the tutor's hands and kicking the table.)

options.A: Implement strategies to increase predictability and scheduling within the classroom at an individual level, such as an object or picture schedule illustrating the daily routine.
options.B: Provide opportunities for the student to voice opinions about the sorting task, ensuring their perspectives are valued through a dialogic pedagogy approach.
options.C: Implement speech-generating devices to enable the student to verbally identify or 'read aloud' the names of the objects being sorted when called upon.
options.D: Engage the student in alternative table work activities such as drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making instead of the routine sorting task.
